title: Master Stability Functions for Synchronized Coupled Systems
claim_supported: Transverse stability of a network's synchronous state factorizes into an MSF at the Laplacian eigenvalues.
relation_to_project: The exact tool the seed paper uses; anchors our transverse-stability metrics.
difference_from_project: We apply the idea to time-varying (switched) coupling and to a financial basis system, not a fixed graph.

title: Synchronization in chaotic systems
claim_supported: Coupled chaotic systems synchronize when transverse (conditional) Lyapunov exponents are negative.
relation_to_project: Establishes the Lyapunov criterion underlying our G0 chaos check and transverse exponents.
difference_from_project: We study switched, sparse coupling and its finite-time analogue, not static bidirectional coupling.

title: Synchronization in Small-World Systems
claim_supported: Synchronizability is governed by the Laplacian eigenratio, improved by shortcuts.
relation_to_project: Motivates why sparse-but-rewired topologies can synchronize.
difference_from_project: We test whether temporal redistribution (not structural shortcuts) explains contraction, and demote lambda-ratio metrics.

authors: Huang, L.; Chen, Q.; Lai, Y.-C.; Pecora, L.M.
title: Generic behavior of master-stability functions in coupled nonlinear dynamical systems
claim_supported: Classifies generic MSF shapes (unbounded vs bounded stable window).
relation_to_project: Tells us which coupling regimes admit a bounded stable window.
difference_from_project: We do not assume a specific MSF class; we compute transverse exponents numerically for switched coupling.

authors: Arenas, A.; Diaz-Guilera, A.; Kurths, J.; Moreno, Y.; Zhou, C.
title: Synchronization in complex networks
claim_supported: Review linking topology spectra to synchronizability.
relation_to_project: Background mapping topology to synchronizability.
difference_from_project: A review of static networks; we focus on temporal order and identifiability from data.

authors: Belykh, I.V.; Belykh, V.N.; Hasler, M.
title: Blinking model and synchronization in small-world networks with a time-varying coupling
claim_supported: Fast on/off (blinking) links synchronize as if coupled by the time-averaged topology.
relation_to_project: Core theory explaining why fast switching approaches the average graph (our G1/G2 finding).
difference_from_project: We quantify the finite-rate gap to the average graph and ask if it is identifiable in markets.

authors: Stilwell, D.J.; Bollt, E.M.; Roberson, D.G.
title: Sufficient Conditions for Fast Switching Synchronization in Time-Varying Network Topologies
claim_supported: If the time-averaged network synchronizes, fast switching guarantees synchronization.
relation_to_project: The averaging theorem behind 'faster switching -> synchronization'.
difference_from_project: Our surrogate shows switching never BEATS the average graph, consistent with this; we test identifiability, which they do not.

authors: Hasler, M.; Belykh, V.; Belykh, I.
title: Dynamics of Stochastically Blinking Systems. Part I: Finite Time Properties
claim_supported: Quantifies finite-time deviations of a blinking system from its averaged system.
relation_to_project: Bounds how far finite-rate switching departs from the average (our finite-rate gap).
difference_from_project: We measure this gap empirically and in a financial basis system, not the general theory.

authors: Porfiri, M.; Stilwell, D.J.; Bollt, E.M.; Skufca, J.D.
title: Random talk: Random walk and synchronizability in a moving neighborhood network
claim_supported: Time-varying neighborhoods synchronize under fast-switching averaging.
relation_to_project: Extends averaging to proximity-driven time-varying links.
difference_from_project: Our links are arbitrage channels on mirror assets, and we address estimation from observations.

authors: Frasca, M.; Buscarino, A.; Rizzo, A.; Fortuna, L.; Boccaletti, S.
title: Synchronization of Moving Chaotic Agents
claim_supported: Mobile agents synchronize when they move fast relative to intrinsic dynamics.
relation_to_project: Confirms the timescale-separation principle we rely on.
difference_from_project: We map 'fast' to channel-rotation rate and test whether order matters beyond rate.

authors: Jadbabaie, A.; Lin, J.; Morse, A.S.
title: Coordination of groups of mobile autonomous agents using nearest neighbor rules
claim_supported: Consensus if the union of switching graphs is jointly connected frequently enough.
relation_to_project: Discrete counterpart of switching-induced synchronization; grounds our joint-connectivity metric.
difference_from_project: We use joint connectivity/reachability as controls and study nonlinear + financial systems.

title: Consensus Problems in Networks of Agents With Switching Topology and Time-Delays
claim_supported: Consensus preserved under switching topologies when graphs stay connected/balanced.
relation_to_project: Lyapunov/algebraic-connectivity tools for switching graphs.
difference_from_project: We show algebraic connectivity of the AVERAGE graph, not switching per se, explains our contraction.

title: Stability of multiagent systems with time-dependent communication links
claim_supported: Set-contraction arguments give consensus under general uniformly-connected links.
relation_to_project: Most general switching-consensus condition; underpins robustness (G3).
difference_from_project: Linear consensus theory; we add chaotic nodes and estimation error.

title: Consensus seeking in multiagent systems under dynamically changing interaction topologies
claim_supported: Consensus requires only joint connectivity over time, not at every instant.
relation_to_project: Directly analogous to sparse-but-switching contraction.
difference_from_project: We contrast joint connectivity with the average graph and test financial identifiability.

authors: Olfati-Saber, R.; Fax, J.A.; Murray, R.M.
title: Consensus and Cooperation in Networked Multi-Agent Systems
claim_supported: Survey unifying consensus across fixed/switching/delayed networks via Laplacian spectra.
relation_to_project: Bridges control-theory consensus to physics synchronization for our framing.
difference_from_project: A survey; our contribution is the financial translation and identifiability test.

authors: Schroder, M.; Mannattil, M.; Dutta, D.; Chakraborty, S.; Timme, M.
title: Transient Uncoupling Induces Synchronization
claim_supported: Intermittently switching coupling off in state-space regions enlarges the sync basin.
relation_to_project: Seed reference; 'less coupling can help' parallels sparse switched links.
difference_from_project: We switch link TOPOLOGY (which pairs), not state-space regions, and target markets.

authors: Boccaletti, S.; Hwang, D.-U.; Chavez, M.; Amann, A.; Kurths, J.; Pecora, L.M.
title: Synchronization in dynamical networks: Evolution along commutative graphs
claim_supported: When successive coupling matrices commute, MSF extends exactly to time-varying networks.
relation_to_project: The exact (commuting) case our order-sensitive propagator generalizes.
difference_from_project: Our schedules are non-commuting; we measure the order effect the commuting case hides.

authors: Kohar, V.; Ji, P.; Choudhary, A.; Sinha, S.; Kurths, J.
title: Synchronization in time-varying networks
claim_supported: Rapidly switching links enhance synchronization vs the static counterpart.
relation_to_project: Seed reference; empirical basis for switching-speed dependence.
difference_from_project: We add causal controls (occupancy/order/coverage) and financial identifiability.

authors: Zhou, J.; Guo, X.; Liu, Z.; Lai, Y.-C.; Lin, W.
title: Random temporal connections promote network synchronization
claim_supported: Random time-varying links improve synchronizability of poorly connected networks.
relation_to_project: Seed reference for the sparse-plus-switching mechanism.
difference_from_project: We ask whether the effect exceeds the average graph and is estimable from data.

authors: Ghosh, D.; Frasca, M.; Rizzo, A.; Majhi, S.; Rakshit, S.; Alfaro-Bittner, K.; Boccaletti, S.
title: The synchronized dynamics of time-varying networks
claim_supported: Comprehensive review of synchronization across temporal/blinking/adaptive networks.
relation_to_project: Definitive survey situating the seed paper and our vocabulary.
difference_from_project: A review; our contribution is the financial mapping and identifiability discipline.

authors: Eser, M.C.; Medeiros, E.S.; Riza, M.; Zakharova, A.
title: Edges of inter-layer synchronization in multilayer networks with time-switching links
claim_supported: Inter-layer synchronization depends on the number of switching cross-links and timescale.
relation_to_project: Direct predecessor establishing the two-layer FHN switching setup we reproduce.
difference_from_project: We reproduce independently, add causal decomposition, and attempt the financial translation.

authors: Jardon-Kojakhmetov, H.; Kuehn, C.; Longo, I.P.
title: Persistent synchronization of heterogeneous networks with time-dependent linear diffusive coupling
claim_supported: Rigorous conditions for persistent synchronization under time-dependent diffusive coupling.
relation_to_project: Math backbone for time-dependent coupling in our linear surrogate.
difference_from_project: We add heterogeneity/directed/signed stress tests and estimation from observations.

authors: Eser, M.C.; Medeiros, E.S.; Riza, M.; Engel, M.
title: Dynamic link switching induces stable synchronized states in sparse networks
claim_supported: Switching sparse cross-layer links induces stable inter-layer synchronization; faster is more effective.
relation_to_project: The SEED paper whose mechanism we audit, reproduce, and try to translate.
difference_from_project: We add causal isolation, robustness, identifiability, and the financial reading it does not claim.

title: Temporal networks
claim_supported: Foundational review: temporal networks, time-respecting paths, reachability.
relation_to_project: Formal language for our temporal reachability and joint connectivity metrics.
difference_from_project: We connect temporal reachability to transverse contraction, which they do not.

title: Modern temporal network theory: a colloquium
claim_supported: Updates temporal-network measures, models, and processes.
relation_to_project: Bridges temporal-network structure to dynamics.
difference_from_project: We apply it to a synchronization/arbitrage-channel setting.

title: Path lengths, correlations, and centrality in temporal networks
claim_supported: Defines temporal path length; temporal correlations slow reachability.
relation_to_project: Justifies order-sensitivity of reachability (our H3).
difference_from_project: We tie reachability to a contraction operator, not spreading.

title: Network reachability of real-world contact sequences
claim_supported: Time-ordering of contacts sharply limits who can influence whom.
relation_to_project: Establishes time-respecting reachability, central to our node-sweep critique.
difference_from_project: We use it as a control showing coverage != reachability.

title: Measuring Financial Asset Return and Volatility Spillovers, with Application to Global Equity Markets
claim_supported: VAR variance decompositions yield a scalar spillover/connectedness index.
relation_to_project: Candidate financial network we contrast with switching structure.
difference_from_project: Connectedness is a correlational/VAR object; we separate it from switching-induced contraction.

title: Better to give than to receive: Predictive directional measurement of volatility spillovers
claim_supported: Generalized variance decompositions give directional net spillovers.
relation_to_project: Directional measures mirror lead-lag direction in our networks.
difference_from_project: Directional != causal transverse coupling; we keep the distinction (interpretation discipline).

title: On the network topology of variance decompositions: Measuring the connectedness of financial firms
claim_supported: Recasts variance-decomposition connectedness as a weighted directed network.
relation_to_project: The 'VAR = network' equivalence enabling graph tools on markets.
difference_from_project: We warn a nonzero VAR edge is not a causal link (discipline).

authors: Billio, M.; Getmansky, M.; Lo, A.W.; Pelizzon, L.
title: Econometric measures of connectedness and systemic risk in the finance and insurance sectors
claim_supported: Granger-causality/PCA networks densify before crises.
relation_to_project: Evidence financial networks are time-varying and densify in stress.
difference_from_project: We test whether temporal ORDER of that densification matters beyond density.

title: A network analysis of the volatility of high-dimensional financial series
venue: J. Royal Statistical Society Series C 66, 581
claim_supported: Long-run partial-correlation volatility networks after removing common factors.
relation_to_project: Method for the idiosyncratic network after factor removal (our G4 confound control).
difference_from_project: We use factor removal as a mandatory baseline, not the end product.

title: NETS: Network estimation for time series
claim_supported: Jointly estimates contemporaneous and Granger networks in large panels via LASSO.
relation_to_project: Candidate estimator to recover the market network (P1.4).
difference_from_project: We test whether ANY such estimator can recover switching vs estimator-induced turnover.

authors: Fan, J.; Liao, Y.; Mincheva, M.
title: Large covariance estimation by thresholding principal orthogonal complements
venue: J. Royal Statistical Society Series B 75, 603
claim_supported: Separates a low-rank factor part from a sparse residual (POET).
relation_to_project: Justifies factor removal isolating the idiosyncratic network.
difference_from_project: We show in the surrogate that venue-differencing removes the factor without POET.

title: Regularized estimation in sparse high-dimensional time series models
claim_supported: Consistency of regularized estimators for high-dimensional stable VAR.
relation_to_project: Theory for estimating large switching VAR networks.
difference_from_project: Assumes stability; our basis system is intentionally near-unstable between switches.

title: Oracle inequalities for high dimensional vector autoregressions
claim_supported: Oracle inequalities for LASSO estimation of high-dimensional VAR.
relation_to_project: Bounds estimation error of the network adjacency (false links).
difference_from_project: We measure false-link rates directly rather than assume oracle behavior.

title: One Security, Many Markets: Determining the Contributions to Price Discovery
claim_supported: Information share attributes efficient-price innovation variance across venues.
relation_to_project: Defines the price-discovery meaning of inter-venue coupling (T1).
difference_from_project: We separate price discovery from synchronization/contraction (discipline).

title: Estimation of Common Long-Memory Components in Cointegrated Systems
claim_supported: Decomposes cointegrated prices into permanent and transitory components.
relation_to_project: Basis of our 'sync manifold = cointegrating relation' reading.
difference_from_project: We use it conceptually; no real cointegrated data are touched in P1.

title: Intraday Price Formation in U.S. Equity Index Markets
claim_supported: Shows which index instrument dominates high-frequency price discovery.
relation_to_project: Template for spot vs perp leadership (T1).
difference_from_project: Empirical equities; we are synthetic-only in P1.

authors: Baillie, R.T.; Booth, G.G.; Tse, Y.; Zabotina, T.
title: Price discovery and common factor models
claim_supported: Relates Hasbrouck information shares to Gonzalo-Granger common-factor weights.
relation_to_project: Reconciles the two price-discovery measures we would report.
difference_from_project: Methodological; we cite for interpretation discipline.

authors: Alexander, C.; Choi, J.; Park, H.; Sohn, S.
title: BitMEX bitcoin derivatives: Price discovery, informational efficiency, and hedging effectiveness
claim_supported: The perpetual swap leads price discovery over spot.
relation_to_project: Evidences the spot-perp lead-lag we would model (T1).
difference_from_project: We only motivate the empirical stage; no crypto data used in P1.

title: Price discovery in bitcoin spot or futures?
claim_supported: Bitcoin spot leads CME/CBOE futures (Hasbrouck/Gonzalo-Granger).
relation_to_project: Shows price-discovery methods on crypto spot-futures.
difference_from_project: Leadership varies by venue/period; we treat that as the switching phenomenon.

title: Price discovery in Bitcoin: The impact of unregulated markets
claim_supported: Unregulated perpetual/futures venues dominate bitcoin price discovery.
relation_to_project: Motivates including funding-linked perp venues.
difference_from_project: Empirical; our P1 is synthetic.

authors: Entrop, O.; Frijns, B.; Seruset, M.
title: The determinants of price discovery on bitcoin markets
claim_supported: Spot-vs-futures price-discovery share varies over time with costs/volume.
relation_to_project: Time-variation of leadership = the switching we translate.
difference_from_project: We ask if that time-variation is more than density/factor-driven.

title: Comovements in Stock Prices in the Very Short Run
venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association 74, 291
claim_supported: Measured cross-correlations fall toward zero as sampling shrinks (Epps effect).
relation_to_project: Warns HF sync estimates are biased by asynchrony; drives our async control.
difference_from_project: We reproduce Epps-like degradation of identifiability in the surrogate (G4).

title: On covariance estimation of non-synchronously observed diffusion processes
claim_supported: Consistent covariance from non-synchronous ticks without interpolation.
relation_to_project: Estimator to measure coupling from asynchronous ticks.
difference_from_project: We show even correct estimators cannot separate switching once synchronized.

title: Estimating betas from nonsynchronous data
claim_supported: Non-synchronous trading biases OLS betas/correlations.
relation_to_project: Classic nonsynchronous-trading correction for lead-lag.
difference_from_project: We use event-time controls rather than beta correction.

authors: Barndorff-Nielsen, O.E.; Hansen, P.R.; Lunde, A.; Shephard, N.
title: Multivariate realised kernels: consistent PSD estimators of the covariation of equity prices with noise and non-synchronous trading
claim_supported: Realised-kernel estimators give PSD covariance robust to noise/asynchrony.
relation_to_project: PSD covariance input to any network computation.
difference_from_project: We do not require PSD matrices; our metric is a contraction operator.

title: Dynamic Conditional Correlation
claim_supported: DCC-GARCH models time-varying conditional correlations parsimoniously.
relation_to_project: Workhorse for time-varying coupling strength between markets.
difference_from_project: Correlation dynamics != switching topology; we separate them (discipline).

title: Extreme Correlation of International Equity Markets
claim_supported: Correlations rise in bear-market extremes, asymmetrically.
relation_to_project: Evidence coupling intensifies in crises (the onset we would detect).
difference_from_project: We caution rising correlation may be common-factor domination, not switching.

title: Asymmetric correlations of equity portfolios
claim_supported: Downside correlations exceed upside, especially in declines.
relation_to_project: Regime-dependent coupling asymmetry a model must reproduce.
difference_from_project: We do not model returns' sign asymmetry; we model transverse contraction.

title: No Contagion, Only Interdependence: Measuring Stock Market Comovements
claim_supported: After heteroskedasticity correction, crisis 'contagion' is largely stable interdependence.
relation_to_project: Critical caution: rising measured correlation can be a volatility artifact.
difference_from_project: Directly supports our discipline separating contagion from correlation from synchronization.

title: Designing temporal networks that synchronize under resource constraints
claim_supported: Temporal networks can achieve stable synchronization under an edge/resource budget where NO static network can; the effect is linked to MSF curvature and appears at intermediate switching rates.
relation_to_project: Direct counterpoint to a naive averaging reading: motivates the separate intermediate-rate temporal-advantage branch and forbids concluding 'switching never beats static'.
difference_from_project: They DESIGN advantageous temporal networks under a budget; we ask whether such an advantage is identifiable from observed financial series (a distinct, harder question).
